explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: The Viatris 10-K Annual Report (FY2024) references governance considerations.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: The Viatris 10-K Annual Report (FY2024) provides evidence for the **governance** and **privacy** pillars of responsible AI. This annual report discusses the risks associated with AI use concerning data privacy and confidential information, highlighting the necessity for robust governance frameworks in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.
relevant_sources.title: Viatris 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: The Viatris 10-K Annual Report (FY2024) references privacy considerations. Additionally, the Viatris Privacy Notice describes the collection, use, disclosure, and retention of personal data. This notice also mentions inferences about population segments and browsing activity, stating these are based on data such as Commercial Data and Health Data.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

relevant_sources.summary: The Viatris 10-K Annual Report (FY2024) provides evidence for the **governance** and **privacy** pillars of responsible AI. This annual report discusses the risks associated with AI use concerning data privacy and confidential information, highlighting the necessity for robust governance frameworks in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.
relevant_sources.title: Viatris 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)

relevant_sources.summary: This Viatris Privacy Notice, a company-owned privacy policy, provides evidence for the **privacy** pillar. The notice describes the collection, use, disclosure, and retention of personal data, and specifically mentions "inferences" about "population segments" and "browsing activity" based on data including "Commercial Data" and "Health Data." This indicates potential automated profiling practices, which are relevant to privacy considerations.
relevant_sources.title: Viatris Privacy Notice

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: Drawing from 11 publicly available sources, Viatris' published materials address 2 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. These include privacy, where the Viatris Privacy Notice describes the collection, use, disclosure, and retention of personal data, and governance, referenced in the Viatris 10-K Annual Report (FY2024). No qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
